terms.definition: A Seaborn function (e.g. relplot, catplot, pairplot) that creates and manages an entire Matplotlib figure, often with faceting via col/row parameters.
A Seaborn function (e.g. scatterplot, histplot, heatmap) that draws on the current axes or a supplied ax= without owning the full figure layout.
A column name passed to color or style plot elements by category, enabling group comparisons on the same axes.
A layout of small multiples split by row and/or column variable values, created by figure-level functions with row= and col=.
A smooth curve approximating a variable's probability density; often overlaid on histograms via kde=True in histplot or displot.
